barRaise.topFinding: BLOCK stands a ninth run and is wider than believed: seven public pages link the gated mirrors, not the three tracked since 2026-07-07, so the fix carried for eight runs would have left four links live and not cleared it. Two corrections outweigh any regression this run. A genuinely new exposure: internal working artifacts tracked at the repo root and in a hidden results tree are publicly fetchable and not robots-covered, because the ignore rules were added after the files were tracked and so have never bound. Nothing sensitive is in them and a sweep of 484 tracked files found zero credential values, so one untrack pass closes it. The chronic-findings story now has a verified root cause, and it is not discipline: this project's bar-raise has never enqueued a finding, while 51 bar-raise items sit queued for other projects and this project's own 15 queued items drain at 13 of 15. Both halves of the machine work and were never connected here. Also proven this run: the mojibake blindspot is live rather than theoretical, in two tracked files, traced to cp1252 JSON reads. Three standing claims were disproved and are recorded as corrections in the report: the GDD payload guard exists upstream, queue appends are atomic, and the contrast failure also affects the homepage. Health holds at 67. Reliability and dependency rose mainly because standing findings were overstated, while security, solo-tool-ux and data-integrity fell on real and previously unmeasured things. Exposure specifics are kept out of this public file and live only in the gitignored report.

actions.title: Untrack the internal working artifacts tracked at the repo root and in a hidden results tree so the existing ignore rules bind, then redeploy and verify each is no longer fetchable
actions.evidence: ignore rules exist but the files were tracked first, so they never bound
